original: Michael Larson shown winning record $110,237 on American TV game show "Press Your Luck" by memorizing patterns
"Jurassic Park," directed bySteven Spielbergand starring Sam Neill and Jeff Goldblum, opens and sets a box office weekend record of $502 million
Chicago Cubs (7) & NY Mets (4) tie record of 11 HRs in a game
"Sloop John B" by The Beach Boys hits #1 in UK
Main Street Electrical Parade premieres in the Magic Kingdom at Walt Disney World
Christa Tybus of London sets record of 24½ hrs of hula-hoop
Continental Congress creates committee to draft a Declaration of Independence withThomas Jefferson, John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Roger Sherman, and Robert R. Livingston as members
"(I'm A) Road Runner" by Jr Walker & The All-Stars peaks at #20
Russian space probe Vega 1 lands on Venus
Future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruthhits 19th & 20th homers of MLB season record 60 HRs in New York's 6-4 win over Cleveland Indians at Yankees Stadium
"E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial", directed bySteven Spielberg, starring Henry Thomas andDrew Barrymore, is released
MLB Texas RangersNolan Ryanpitches his 6th no-hitter beating Oakland, in the 9th inning, he retires Ken Phelps,Rickey HendersonandWillie Randolph
Saunders-Roe SR.N1, the first practical hovercraft, performs its first public flight
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedysays segregation is morally wrong and that it is "time to act"
31°F lowest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland, Ohio in June
Cassini-Huygens makes its closest flyby of Phoebe
Tiffany Cohen, American swimmer (Olympic gold 400m, 800m freestyle 1984), born in Culver City, California

Texas mother hit by lightning while standing in her kitchen in her Texas home. Witnesses say lightning came through a light fixture, struck her chest and exited her foot. Survived after three days in hospital.
Lillian "Pokey" Watson, American swimmer (Olympic gold 4 x 100 relay 1964, 200m backstroke 1968), born in Mineola, New York
Sandra Schmirler, Canadian curler (Olympic gold team 1998; World C'ship gold 1993, 94, 87), born in Biggar, Saskatchewan, Canada (d. 2000)
Benjamin Franklininvents his Franklin stove
British ruler KingGeorge VIand Queen Elizabeth taste their first "hot dogs" at a party atFDR's Hyde Park residence during their US visit
Perennial Christmas film "Miracle on 34th St" starring John Payne andMaureen O'Hara, directed by George Seaton is released
Charles Fabry, French physicist, discoverer of ozone layer in upper atmosphere, born in Marseille, France (d. 1945)
Erika Salumäe, Estonian track cyclist (first Olympic gold medal for Estonia; women's sprint 1988 USSR, 92; World C'ship gold 1987, 89), born in Pärnu, Estonia
Diplomatic dispute at Tang Chinese capital Chang'an when Abbasid Arabs and Uyghur Turks both arrive to offer tribute. Settlement reached when both enter at same time through two different gates.
Florentine scientist Evangelista Torricelli describes his invention of the mercury barometer in 1643 in a letter to Michelangelo Ricci
Charles Duryea patents a gas-driven automobile
Pennsylvania Railroad debuts the fastest train in the world, capable of traveling from NY to Chicago in 18 hours
1st magnesium jet airplane flies
Chicago police break up Rolling Stones press conference on a traffic island in the middle of Michigan Avenue
One-day international cricket debut ofJaved Miandadat age 17 years and 364 days
US Olympic baseball team plays 1st exhibition game, beat Venezuela 20-0
Cbox is 39'115 long, 20'85 wide, & 8' high in Jacksonville, Florida - largest popcorn container is 6,619.76 cubic feet full of popped corn
The World Health Organization declares H1N1 swine flu to be a global pandemic, the first such incident in over forty years
Johann Georg Palitzsch, German astronomer, 1st saw Halley's comet on return, born in Prohlis, Electorate of Saxony, Holy Roman Empire (now Germany) (d. 1788)
Dai Vernon [David Frederick Wingfield Verner], Canadian magician, The Professor, born in Ottawa, Ontario (d. 1992)
Four Days Battle - naval battle in the southern North Seas during Second Anglo-Dutch War: Dutch victory with ten English ships destroyed [OS 1-4 June]
NY Times reports the NYC subway will auction off unclaimed items left behind on the trains
Trojan War: Troy is sacked and burned according to calculations by Eratosthenes
Inventor Edwin Armstrong gives the first public demonstration of FM broadcasting in the United States, at Alpine, New Jersey
Manfred Mann record Do Wah Diddy Diddy Dum Diddy Do
"The Ballad OfJohn & Yoko" by The Beatles hits #1 in UK; recordinginvolved only John(guitars, vocals, percussion) and Paul (bass, drums, piano, backing voice)
Defending championLarry HolmesTKOs Gerry Cooney in 13 for WBC and The Ring heavyweight boxing titles at Caesars Palace, LV, Nevada
Natalie Colereleases "Unforgettable...with Love", covering her father's standards including a "virtual duet" of title track; wins 6 Grammy Awards, including Album of the Year, 1992

Canadian Prime MinisterStephen Harpermade an official historic apology to Canada's First Nations in regard to a residential school abuse in which children were isolated from their homes, families and cultures for a century
Carl von Linde, German engineer and chemist who invented mechanical refrigeration, born in Berndorf, Germany (d. 1934)
Millicent Fawcett, English leader of women's rights, born in London (d. 1929)
Anton Hildebrand, Dutch children's book writer (Man in the Moon), born in Groningen, Netherlands (d. 1977)
Rachel Heyhoe Flint, English cricket batter (22 Tests, 23 ODOs; captain 1966-78; inaugural Women's World Cup 1973), businesswoman and philanthropist, born in Wolverhampton, England (d. 2017)
James "Ox" van Hoften, American astronaut (STS 11, STS 20), born in Fresno, California
Beatles "Rock & Roll Music" compilation LP is released in USA

Emperor Taizong of Tang sends envoys to the Xueyantuo bearing gold and silk for the release of Chinese prisoners captured on the northern frontier during the transition from the Sui to Tang dynasty and succeeds in freeing 80,000
Smoke Monkey ascends to the throne of the Maya city of Copán, after the capture and killing of his predecessor 18 Rabbit by rival city Quirigua
Basilica of St Denis is dedicated near Paris, the first fully Gothic church
Jews of Algiers escape attacking Spanish Army
Construction of the first British ship on the Pacific coast begins at Nootka Sound, British Columbia
Puerto Rican flag adopted
George Dennett, aided by Gilbert Jessop, dismisses Northamptonshire for 12 runs, the lowest total in first-class cricket
Brazil adopts women's suffrage
